content.body: Chemical bonds form when atoms interact to achieve a stable electron configuration — typically a full outer shell (octet rule, 8 electrons). There are three main types of chemical bonds: ionic, covalent, and metallic. The type of bond determines the physical and chemical properties of the substance.

IONIC BONDING:
Formed between a METAL and a NON-METAL
Metal loses electrons → becomes positive ion (cation)
Non-metal gains electrons → becomes negative ion (anion)
Opposite charges attract → ionic bond

Electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions

Example:
Na (2,8,1) → loses 1e⁻ → Na⁺ (2,8)
Cl (2,8,7) → gains 1e⁻ → Cl⁻ (2,8,8)
Na⁺ and Cl⁻ form NaCl (sodium chloride = table salt)

Properties of Ionic Compounds:
• High melting and boiling points (strong electrostatic forces)
• Solid at room temperature (giant ionic lattice structure)
• Conduct electricity when DISSOLVED in water or MOLTEN (ions free to move)
• Do NOT conduct when solid (ions fixed in lattice)
• Usually soluble in water
• Hard but brittle (layers shift under pressure, like charges repel)

content.title: Dot-and-Cross Diagrams
content.body:
Sodium chloride (NaCl) formation:
Na has 1 outer electron — it TRANSFERS to Cl
Cl has 7 outer electrons — receives 1 to complete octet
Na becomes Na⁺ (like Ne configuration: 2,8)
Cl becomes Cl⁻ (like Ar configuration: 2,8,8)

Magnesium oxide (MgO):
Mg (2,8,2) → loses 2e⁻ → Mg²⁺
O (2,6) → gains 2e⁻ → O²⁻
Formula: MgO (charges balance 2+ and 2-)

questions.question: Ionic bonds form between:
questions.explanation: Ionic bonds form when a metal (donates electrons) bonds with a non-metal (accepts electrons).

questions.question: Na loses 1 electron. The ion formed is:
questions.explanation: Losing one electron gives Na a positive charge of +1. Ion = Na⁺.

questions.question: Ionic compounds conduct electricity when:
questions.explanation: Ionic compounds only conduct when ions are free to move — in molten state or solution. Solid ionic compounds have fixed ions and don't conduct.

questions.question: Why do ionic compounds have high melting points?
options: Strong electrostatic attraction between ions requires much energy to break
questions.answer: Strong electrostatic attraction between ions requires much energy to break
questions.explanation: The giant ionic lattice has strong forces between all ions. Much energy is needed to overcome these forces and separate the ions.

questions.question: Magnesium ion Mg²⁺ is formed when Mg:
questions.explanation: Mg has 2 outer electrons. It loses both to form Mg²⁺ with a full outer shell.

questions.question: Chloride ion Cl⁻ has the same electron configuration as:
questions.explanation: Cl⁻ has 2,8,8 — same as argon (Ar). Both have 18 electrons.

questions.question: Formula of calcium oxide: Ca²⁺ and O²⁻
questions.explanation: Ca²⁺ (+2) and O²⁻ (-2). Charges balance at ratio 1:1. Formula = CaO.

questions.question: Ionic compounds are brittle because:
options: Layers shift under pressure — like charges align and repel
questions.answer: Layers shift under pressure — like charges align and repel
questions.explanation: When force is applied, ionic layers shift. Like charges end up adjacent — massive repulsion causes the crystal to shatter.

questions.question: The octet rule states atoms are most stable with:
questions.explanation: Most atoms are most stable with 8 electrons in their outer shell (except H and He which need 2).

questions.question: Which is an ionic compound?
questions.explanation: NaCl (sodium chloride) is formed between Na (metal) and Cl (non-metal) — it is ionic.

content.body:
COVALENT BONDING:
Formed between TWO NON-METALS
Atoms SHARE pairs of electrons (not transfer)
Each atom provides one electron to each shared pair
Single bond: 1 shared pair (—)
Double bond: 2 shared pairs (=)
Triple bond: 3 shared pairs (≡)

content.title: Simple Covalent Molecules
content.body:
Hydrogen (H₂): H–H (1 single bond)
Chlorine (Cl₂): Cl–Cl
Water (H₂O): 2 O–H bonds, O has 2 lone pairs
Ammonia (NH₃): 3 N–H bonds, N has 1 lone pair
Methane (CH₄): 4 C–H bonds, tetrahedral shape
Carbon dioxide (CO₂): O=C=O (2 double bonds)

Properties of Simple Covalent Compounds:
• Low melting and boiling points (weak intermolecular forces between molecules)
• Usually gases or liquids at room temperature
• Do NOT conduct electricity (no charged particles)
• Insoluble in water (usually) but soluble in organic solvents

content.title: Giant Covalent Structures
content.body:
Some covalent substances form giant lattices:

Diamond (carbon):
• Each C bonded to 4 others (tetrahedral)
• Very hard (all bonds must be broken)
• Very high melting point
• Does NOT conduct electricity

Silicon dioxide (SiO₂) — quartz/sand:
• Giant covalent lattice
• Very hard, high melting point

Graphite (carbon):
• Layers of hexagonal rings
• Conducts electricity (1 free electron per carbon)
• Soft and slippery (layers slide over each other)
• Used as lubricant and in pencils

questions.question: Covalent bonds involve:
questions.explanation: In covalent bonding, non-metal atoms share electron pairs to achieve full outer shells.

questions.question: H₂O has how many covalent bonds?
questions.explanation: Water: H–O–H. Two O–H single covalent bonds.

questions.question: CO₂ structure is:
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide: two C=O double bonds. Carbon forms 4 bonds total (2 double bonds = 4).

questions.question: Why do simple covalent compounds have low melting points?
options: Weak intermolecular forces between molecules need little energy to overcome
questions.answer: Weak intermolecular forces between molecules need little energy to overcome
questions.explanation: The COVALENT bonds within molecules are strong. But the forces BETWEEN molecules (intermolecular forces) are weak — these are broken on melting.

questions.question: Diamond is hard because:
options: Each carbon forms 4 strong covalent bonds in a giant 3D lattice
questions.answer: Each carbon forms 4 strong covalent bonds in a giant 3D lattice
questions.explanation: Diamond's giant covalent lattice — every atom bonded to 4 others by strong covalent bonds — makes it extremely hard.

questions.question: Graphite conducts electricity because:
options: One electron per carbon is delocalized and free to move
questions.answer: One electron per carbon is delocalized and free to move
questions.explanation: In graphite, each C forms 3 bonds. The 4th electron is delocalized (free) — these mobile electrons conduct electricity.

questions.question: Which is a covalent compound?
questions.explanation: H₂O is formed between two non-metals (H and O) — covalent bonding.

questions.question: Methane (CH₄) contains:
questions.explanation: Carbon forms 4 single bonds. Methane: C bonded to 4 hydrogen atoms by 4 single C–H bonds.

questions.question: Why doesn't simple covalent H₂O conduct electricity?
options: No free ions or electrons to carry charge
questions.answer: No free ions or electrons to carry charge
questions.explanation: Covalent molecules have no charged particles (ions or free electrons), so they cannot conduct electrical current.

questions.question: Silicon dioxide (SiO₂) has a high melting point because:
options: It has a giant covalent lattice with many strong bonds to break
questions.answer: It has a giant covalent lattice with many strong bonds to break
questions.explanation: SiO₂ (sand/quartz) has a giant covalent structure. Enormous energy is needed to break all the strong covalent bonds throughout the lattice.
